text:
On an ordinary autumn afternoon in Elmwoodvale, nine-year-old Sally loved nothing more than roaming through the cozy alleyways behind her home. Today was no exception.

She climbed up the weathered oak tree at the corner. Suddenly a wise old owl hooted.

"Do tell," the owl whispered.

"I've stumbled upon something extraordinary!" Sally eagerly confessed.

The owl blinked. "Oh?"

Just then a gust blew down petals. Sally watched as a blossom turned gray. She examined closer.

"Yes! That was my grandmother."

"What happened?"

With a tug of sadness, she unfledged. "I was just a little bird. She never cared much for gardens. This? This is her favorite spot."

With tears, Sally flew down.

The old woman glanced up. "Oh dear..."

"How can I help?" she asked.

With a loving hand, she took her. They sat side by side. Years passed. Grandma taught her things; tales about the woods, what stars meant. On
